name: Number of partnerships
explanation: I systematically reviewed the set of AI-related partnerships for Iliad SA and its subsidiaries, confirming only those with direct evidence of an official AI-related partnership within the company, its group, or subsidiary structure. After deduplication (to avoid counting group and subsidiary affiliations twice, and repeated partnerships such as with Mistral AI), and strict application of the definition, I identified 31 unique partnerships. Each partnership involves a formal collaboration (not just commercial product use) and links back to Iliad SA, covering cloud, AI research, enterprise AI use, infrastructure, and solution integration partners. Partnerships were excluded if there was ambiguity in the relationship or lack of AI relevance.

name: Formal governance structure
answer:
iliad SA’s AI oversight is embedded in its existing governance structure:
- Board-level CSR Committee (members: Bertile Burel, Virginie Calmels; chaired by Jacques Veyrat) overseeing ESG impacts relevant to AI and data-center operations, supported by a CSR Steering Committee.
- Audit Committee overseeing risk management, internal control and internal audit, which includes technology/AI risks.
- Data Protection governance led by a Chief Data Protection Officer with a network of DPOs to ensure GDPR-compliant AI lifecycle management (e.g., DPIAs, records of processing, data-subject rights).
- Ethics & Compliance function (Code of Ethics, anti‑corruption program, and whistleblowing channel) to enforce responsible conduct around AI uses.
- Green Finance Committee (Treasury, Sustainability/CSR and Finance leadership) that evaluates and monitors projects, including energy‑intensive AI/data‑center investments.
explanation: The claims indicate (1) a board-level CSR Committee created in late 2022 (members: B. Burel, V. Calmels; chair: J. Veyrat) that oversees ESG matters relevant to AI/data centers; (2) an Audit Committee responsible for risk management and internal audit, covering emerging tech/AI risks; (3) a Data Protection governance system led by a Chief DPO with a network of DPOs for GDPR compliance; (4) an Ethics & Compliance framework with a Code of Ethics, anti-corruption program, and whistleblowing; and (5) a Green Finance Committee including Treasury, Sustainability/CSR, and Finance leaders to evaluate and monitor eligible projects, notably data center/AI infrastructure. These bodies collectively provide oversight for AI strategy, compliance, and lifecycle as described in the provided claims.
definition: Oversight mechanisms and committees for AI strategy, compliance, and lifecycle

name: AI Ethics documentation
answer: No dedicated AI ethics documentation identified. Iliad’s available materials focus on general business ethics and compliance (Code of Ethics updated from 2021 with a 2025 revision, Anti‑Corruption Code of Conduct, conflicts‑of‑interest brochure, Gifts & Hospitality/Invitations policies, whistleblowing systems) and data protection/GDPR policies, but the claims do not mention AI‑specific ethics or Responsible AI guidelines.
explanation: The claims describe a comprehensive business ethics framework: a Code of Ethics (2021, update scheduled for 2025), Anti‑Corruption Code of Conduct, conflicts‑of‑interest guidelines, and Gifts & Hospitality/Invitations policies, plus whistleblowing systems and GDPR/data protection policies [2][3][6][13]. None of the provided claims reference AI‑specific ethics or Responsible AI documentation, so based on the evidence, Iliad has ethics documentation but not AI‑targeted ethics guidelines in the claims set.
definition: Existence and scope of formal AI ethics guidelines and documentation

name: CAPEX & of Total Revenue
explanation: The company’s FY 2024 Annual Financial Report states that consolidated capital expenditure (excluding spectrum payments) was about €2.07 billion, while total consolidated revenue came in at roughly €9.25 billion. Dividing €2.07 billion by €9.25 billion gives 0.224 (or 22.4 %), so capital expenditure represented a little more than 22 % of total revenue in FY 2024.

name: Number of patents since 2020

name: First year of AI deployment
answer: 2023 — In September 2023, Iliad (via its Scaleway subsidiary) deployed an NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD at Datacenter 5 near Paris—comprising 127 DGX H100 systems (1,016 H100 GPUs)—which at the time was billed as Europe’s most powerful cloud‑native AI supercomputer. Following this, Scaleway began marketing a full suite of cloud‑native AI services to customers, marking Iliad’s first operational‑scale AI deployment.
explanation: Multiple company statements document that Iliad’s first at‑scale AI deployment occurred in 2023: on Sep 26, 2023 the Group announced the installation of an NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD at DC5, described as the most powerful cloud‑native AI supercomputer deployed in Europe at that date [21]. The 2023/2024 reporting further specifies the configuration—127 DGX H100 systems totaling 1,016 H100 GPUs—and notes that Scaleway subsequently commercialized a full suite of cloud‑native AI offerings for customers, evidencing operational use rather than a pilot [5][6].
definition: The first publicly documented year in which the company implemented or deployed AI systems at operational scale (e.g., network automation, customer service bots, predictive maintenance)
